2603.16859.title: SocialOmni: Benchmarking Audio-Visual Social Interactivity in Omni Models
2603.16859.paper_summary: Omni-modal large language models (OLMs) redefine human-machine interaction by natively integrating audio, vision, and text. However, existing OLM benchmarks remain anchored to static, accuracy-centric tasks, leaving a critical gap in assessing social interactivity, the fundamental capacity to navigate dynamic cues in natural dialogues. To this end, we propose SocialOmni, a comprehensive benchmark that operationalizes the evaluation of this conversational interactivity across three core dimensions: (i) speaker separation and identification (who is speaking), (ii) interruption timing control (when to interject), and (iii) natural interruption generation (how to phrase the interruption). SocialOmni features 2,000 perception samples and a quality-controlled diagnostic set of 209 interaction-generation instances with strict temporal and contextual constraints, complemented by controlled audio-visual inconsistency scenarios to test model robustness. We benchmarked 12 leading OLMs, which uncovers significant variance in their social-interaction capabilities across models. Furthermore, our analysis reveals a pronounced decoupling between a model's perceptual accuracy and its ability to generate contextually appropriate interruptions, indicating that understanding-centric metrics alone are insufficient to characterize conversational social competence. More encouragingly, these diagnostics from SocialOmni yield actionable signals for bridging the perception-interaction divide in future OLMs.
2603.16859.paper_summary_zh: 全模態大型語言模型（OLMs）透過原生整合音頻、視覺和文本，重新定義了人機互動。然後，現有的OLM基準仍然固定於靜態、以準確性為中心的任務，這在評估社交互動性方面留下了重要的空白，這是導航自然對話中動態提示的基本能力。為此，我們提出了SocialOmni，一個全面的基準，實現了對這種對話互動性的評估，涵蓋三個核心維度：（i）講者分離和識別（誰在講話），（ii）插話時機控制（何時插話），以及（iii）自然插話生成（如何措辭插話）。SocialOmni包含2,000個感知樣本和一組經過質量控制的診斷集，該集包含209個互動生成實例，並具有嚴格的時間和上下文限制，並輔以受控的音視覺不一致場景以測試模型的穩健性。我們對12個領先的OLM進行了基準測試，揭示了它們在社交互動能力上的顯著差異。此外，我們的分析顯示模型的感知準確性與其生成上下文適當插話的能力之間存在明顯的脫鉤，這表明僅僅依賴理解為中心的指標不足以表徵對話社交能力。更令人鼓舞的是，來自SocialOmni的這些診斷提供了可行的信號，以縮小未來OLM中的感知與互動之間的鴻溝。
2603.16859.author: Tianyu Xie et.al.
2603.16859.authors: Tianyu Xie, Jinfa Huang, Yuexiao Ma, Rongfang Luo, Yan Yang, Wang Chen, Yuhui Zeng, Ruize Fang, Yixuan Zou, Xiawu Zheng, Jiebo Luo, Rongrong Ji
